explanation: Plain LB with constant stores; no dependency edge, no cycle, so WEAKEST admits the reorder. Agrees with JMM.
Real data dep on both threads (r+1) lands in sdep; jfCoherence closes the sdep-union-jf cycle. none over-allows, current forbids -- the genuine thin-air mechanism. Agrees with JMM.
Genuine control dep on P0 (guarded store) + data dep on P1; the ctrl edge (isSemantic=true) plus data edge close the cycle. Agrees with JMM.

Both arms of P0's guard write x=1 so the control dep is redundant and JMM allows it. The parser has no brace/else handling, flattens both-arms-write to the SAME structure as genuine TC03, and records ctrl isSemantic=true unconditionally -- so WEAKEST over-approximates to FORBIDDEN. Expected control-dependency limitation (Batty et al. Theorem 2). Sound over-approximation.

Data-dep cycle with a redundant second read of x. The wired candidate is coherence-inconsistent already under none (the contradictory two-read outcome cannot be wired consistently), so WEAKEST forbids it in every config. Agrees with JMM, though via candidate-coherence rather than the sdep mechanism specifically.

The JMM-forbidden behavior is x reading 2, which is UNREACHABLE (x is only ever written 0 or 1) -- a degenerate forbidden. P0's store x=1 is constant (no dependency), so only P1 carries a data edge: a one-sided dependency that closes no cycle. WEAKEST allows the reachable LB candidate under all three configs (even all-deps-semantic). The single-candidate checker cannot pose the unreachable target behavior; the divergence is an encoding/model-design limitation, not a soundness bug.
Genuine control-dependency cycle (both stores guarded on the other thread's value); both ctrl edges are semantic and close the cycle. none over-allows, current forbids. Agrees with JMM.

Redundant-read guard if(r0==r1) + data feedback. Forbidden under none already: the wired candidate is coherence-inconsistent. Agrees with JMM. (The parser also cannot see r0==r1 always holds, so it records the two-register guard as ctrl isSemantic=true.)
Two-thread data-dep cycle with an extra read; the r+1 data edges close the sdep-union-jf cycle. Agrees with JMM.
Three-thread data-dependency chain z<-y<-x<-...<-z closing into a cycle; every hop is a real data edge. none over-allows, current forbids. Agrees with JMM.

Same dep cycle as TC02 but x,y initialised to 1: reading 1 is justified by the initial write, not the cycle. Both reads wire to the initial write of value 1 (copy-form) and no thin-air justification is needed. WEAKEST allows in all configs. Agrees with JMM.

LB with a data dep on P1 plus an independent unconditional store y=1. The independent write supplies value 1 without the cycle, so the reader reads a non-dependent write and no sdep-union-jf cycle closes. WEAKEST allows even under all-deps-semantic. Agrees with JMM.
SC-per-location: observing y=2 then y=1 violates coherence (co: init -> 1 -> 2). Dependency-independent; forbidden in all three configs. Agrees with JMM (the constraint on the pair of reads).

Algebraic-cancellation trap r0-r1+1: the fake-dep detector correctly does NOT fold it (two distinct registers), keeping the data deps semantic. The wired two-read candidate is also coherence-inconsistent under none, so WEAKEST forbids in every config. Agrees with JMM; the key positive is that the detector does not mistake a two-register expression for a fake dep.

Four-thread copy chain x->y->z->x fed by an independent real source P0 writing x=1. The value originates from a genuine unconditional write, not the cycle. WEAKEST allows in all configs. Agrees with JMM. (Wiring note: copies past the first hop default to the initial write.)

Data-dep chain with P2 also writing x=2 unconditionally; the independent write can justify the propagated value. JMM decision (standard): ALLOWED. WEAKEST allows in all configs. Agrees. (Wiring note: copy chain hops past a constant source do not fully wire.)
Data+control-dependency cycle: P0 data dep y<-x, P1 guarded store x with data dep. Both ctrl and data edges lie on the cycle; none over-allows, current forbids. Agrees with JMM.
Three-thread structure where P1 writes x=1 unconditionally, supplying a non-dependent justification. JMM decision (standard): ALLOWED. WEAKEST allows in all configs. Agrees.

Control deps present, but P0's guard tests r0==0 (the initial value of y), so P0's store fires from the initial state -- no self-justifying loop. The exists names P0 reading initial y=0 and P1 reading x=1, wired faithfully with no closed cycle. WEAKEST allows even with ctrl edges semantic. Agrees with JMM.

LB with both a control and a data dep on P0 (guarded store x=r+1) plus a data dep on P1. Store value and occurrence both depend on the load; the cycle closes. none over-allows, current forbids. Agrees with JMM.
